Index of Female Divinities and Goddess Counterparts in the Bible
Alexander the Great in the Bible
Archangel Michael: Names, Meanings, and References Across Traditions
Hebrew & Aramaic: The name is nearly identical in both languages, reflecting their close relationship.
Theological Significance: The rhetorical question "Who is like God?" underscores Jewish monotheism and God’s supremacy.
Hebrew Bible: Michael is the chief protector of Israel.
New Testament: Michael is the leader of the heavenly armies against Satan.
Dead Sea Scrolls: Michael is "Prince of Light," leading the forces of good.
1 Enoch: Michael is one of the four chief archangels.
